member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.CommandLine.OptionAttribute
member.summary: Attribute to define an option for a command parameter with a description, name, and optional aliases.
member.remarks:
The is used to annotate parameters of methods that represent command-line options.
The name of the option can be set using the property. If not set, the parameter name will be used as the option name.
The property provides a human-readable explanation of the option's purpose and cannot be null.
Also, multiple aliases for the option can be specified using the property or the constructor parameter.

member.summary: Gets the description of the option.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.CommandLine.ConsoleSpinner
member.summary: Provides a console-based spinner animation at a fixed or dynamic position. Useful for indicating progress in console applications.
member.remarks: Thread-safe for concurrent calls to , , and property setters. If Unicode is not supported by the current console (e.g., on some Windows environments or when output encoding is not UTF-8), the spinner gracefully falls back to an ASCII style regardless of the requested spinner style. Spinner character selection is handled at construction time via the GetSpinnerChars method.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.CommandLine.ConsoleSpinner.Color
member.summary: Gets or sets the foreground color used while rendering the spinner (including the optional message).
member.remarks: If not explicitly set, the color at construction time (current ) is used. Setting this property after the spinner has started will affect subsequent frames.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.CommandLine.ConsoleSpinner.PositionX
member.summary: Gets the X (column) position where the spinner is rendered. A value of -1 indicates the current cursor column is used dynamically for every frame.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.CommandLine.ConsoleSpinner.PositionY
member.summary: Gets the Y (row) position where the spinner is rendered. A value of -1 indicates the current cursor row is used dynamically for every frame.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.CommandLine.ConsoleSpinner.Delay
member.summary: Gets the delay in milliseconds between spinner frame updates.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.CommandLine.ConsoleSpinner.IsRunning
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the spinner thread is currently marked as running.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.CommandLine.ConsoleSpinner.Message
member.summary: Gets or sets the message displayed after the spinner character (if any).
member.remarks: Setting this property while the spinner is active updates the message on the next frame.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.CommandLine.ConsoleSpinner.IsUnicodeSupported
member.summary: Determines whether Unicode spinner styles can be rendered in the current console.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.CommandLine.ProgressSpinner
member.summary: Provides a self-contained progress spinner that uses ConsoleUtil's DrawProgressBar functionality. This class allows for easy use with 'using' statements to automatically start and stop progress spinners around long-running operations with unknown duration.
member.remarks: Similar to , this class manages its own background thread and animation but leverages the existing infrastructure with indefinite progress (-1 percentage) to provide consistent visual feedback with the existing progress bar system. The spinner automatically starts when the object is created and stops when disposed.

member.name: F:ByteForge.Toolkit.CommandLine.ProgressSpinner._globalConsoleLock
member.summary: Global lock object to synchronize console access across threads.

member.summary: The thread responsible for running the spinner animation.

member.summary: Indicates whether the spinner is currently running.

member.summary: The current message being displayed with the spinner.

member.summary: Indicates whether the spinner has been disposed.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.CommandLine.ProgressSpinner.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class and starts the spinner.
member.param: The message to display with the spinner. Defaults to "Working...".
member.remarks: The spinner will automatically start displaying when this constructor is called. If no console is available, the spinner will not display but will not cause errors.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.CommandLine.ProgressSpinner.Message
member.summary: Gets or sets the message displayed with the spinner.
member.value: The message to display. Setting this updates the spinner display immediately.
member.remarks: Changing this property while the spinner is active will update the displayed message without stopping and restarting the animation.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.CommandLine.ProgressSpinner.UpdateMessage(System.String)
member.summary: Updates the spinner message to show the current operation.
member.param: The new message to display.
member.remarks: This is a convenience method equivalent to setting the property. If the spinner has been disposed, this method has no effect.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.CommandLine.ProgressSpinner.Stop
member.summary: Stops the progress spinner and clears the display.
member.remarks: This method is automatically called when the object is disposed. It's safe to call this method multiple times.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.CommandLine.ProgressSpinner.Dispose
member.summary: Disposes the ProgressSpinner and stops the animation.
member.remarks: This method implements the IDisposable pattern and automatically stops the spinner when called. It's safe to call multiple times.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.CommandLine.ProgressSpinner.SpinLoop
member.summary: Background thread loop for continuous spinner animation.
member.remarks: This method runs in a background thread and continuously calls with a negative percentage to create the animated spinner effect. The loop continues until is set to false.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.Configuration.ByteForge#Toolkit#Configuration#IConfigurationManager#Save
member.summary: Saves the current configuration settings to the INI file.
member.remarks:
The method updates the INI file to reflect the current state of the in-memory configuration.
It preserves the structure and comments of the existing INI file while adding or updating sections and keys based on the in-memory configuration.
Any keys set to in the configuration are omitted from the file.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.Configuration.ThreadUnsafeSave
member.summary:
Saves the current configuration state to the INI file.
This method is not thread-safe and should not be called concurrently from multiple threads.
member.remarks: This method updates the INI file to reflect the current state of the in-memory configuration. It preserves the structure and comments of the existing INI file while adding or updating sections and keys based on the in-memory configuration. New sections and keys are appended as needed, and keys set to are excluded from the output. Callers must ensure that no other threads are reading or writing to the INI file while this method is executing to avoid data corruption or inconsistent state.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.IParsingHelper
member.summary: Defines parsing operations that transform a textual representation into object instances or populate existing objects / properties using reflection.
member.remarks:
Implementations are expected to:
Support resolving and handling nullable types where appropriate.
Populate only writable, publicly accessible instance members.
Perform defensive checks for null arguments and empty input values.
The contract does not prescribe deep vs. shallow population semantics; that behavior is left to the concrete implementation (e.g., performs a shallow property copy).

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.IParsingHelper.ParseAndPopulateObject(System.Type,System.Object,System.String)
member.summary: Parses the supplied textual into an instance described by (or a compatible intermediary representation) and applies the resulting data onto the existing object.
member.param: The runtime describing the shape expected from the parsed value. Implementations may unwrap nullable types (e.g., Nullable<T>).
The existing object to populate. Must not be null. Implementations typically update writable public instance properties only.
The textual representation to parse. Implementations may no-op if the value is null, empty, or whitespace.
member.exception: Thrown when is null.
Implementations may throw if is invalid or unsupported.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.IParsingHelper.ParseAndPopulateProperty(System.Reflection.PropertyInfo,System.Object,System.String)
member.summary: Parses the supplied textual and assigns (or populates) the specified on the given instance.
member.param: The target property metadata. Must represent a writable property; implementations may validate accessibility or writability.
The object instance that owns the property to be updated. Must not be null.
The textual representation to parse. Implementations may substitute a default value when the input is null or whitespace.
member.exception: Implementations may throw if or is null.
Implementations may throw if the property is not writable or otherwise unsupported.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.ParsingHelper
member.summary:
Provides helper methods that leverage an to parse textual configuration values and populate object instances or property values using reflection. This helper focuses on:
Resolving nullable types to their underlying non-nullable forms.
Creating default instances for properties via DefaultValueHelper.ResolveDefaultValue.
Parsing a string into a temporary object graph and copying writable public properties onto an existing target.
member.remarks: The parsing strategy intentionally performs a shallow copy of public writable instance properties from a parsed temporary instance to the provided target instance, ignoring properties whose parsed value is null.

member.summary: Gets a globally accessible default instance of that uses .

The existing object whose properties will be updated.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.IConfigSection
member.summary: Represents a configuration section that can be loaded and saved from the configuration root.
member.remarks:
This interface is the core of ByteForge's configuration system, defining how configuration sections interact with the configuration storage. It provides a non-generic base for all configuration sections with methods to load and save configuration data.
Configuration sections are typically created and managed by the class, which serves as the configuration root. Each section represents a named collection of related settings (e.g., "Database", "Logging", etc.).
The interface is internal since it's intended for use only within the ByteForge configuration system implementation. External code should use the class to interact with configuration sections.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.IConfigSection.Value
member.summary: Gets the value of the configuration section.
member.remarks:
This property provides access to the configuration data represented by this section. The returned object can be of any type, as determined by the concrete implementation of the interface.
For the generic version , this property returns a strongly-typed value.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.IConfigSection.LoadFromConfiguration
member.summary: Loads the configuration values from the configuration root.
member.remarks:
This method reads values from the underlying configuration storage (typically a file) and populates the property with the loaded data.
The method is called internally by the configuration system when a section is first accessed or when a reload is requested.
Implementations should handle missing sections or properties gracefully, providing default values when appropriate.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.IConfigSection.SaveToConfiguration
member.summary: Saves the current values to the configuration root.
member.remarks:
This method writes the current state of the property back to the underlying configuration storage.
The method is called internally by the configuration system when changes to a section need to be persisted.
Implementations should ensure thread-safety when writing to shared configuration storage.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.IConfigSection`1
member.summary: Represents a configuration section with a specific type that can be loaded and saved from the configuration root.
member.typeparam: The type of the configuration section value.
member.remarks:
This generic interface extends to provide strongly-typed access to configuration values. It's particularly useful for providing intellisense and compile-time type checking when working with configuration objects.
Implementations of this interface typically map a section of the configuration to a specific class or struct, with properties that represent individual configuration settings.
The generic type parameter T is usually a class with properties decorated with to map between configuration keys and class properties.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.IConfigSection`1.Value
member.summary: Gets the value of the configuration section.
member.remarks:
This property provides strongly-typed access to the configuration data represented by this section. The return type is specified by the generic type parameter T.
This property hides the base property and returns the same value but with the specific type T instead of object.
Implementations should ensure that this property never returns null. Default instances should be created for missing sections.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.IConfigurationManager
member.summary: Interface for configuration management providing access to INI-based configuration with strongly-typed sections.
member.remarks: This interface defines the contract for configuration management, enabling both static convenience methods and instance-based testing scenarios. Implementations should support INI file loading, typed section access, array/collection handling, and thread-safe operations.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.ConfigSection`1.ByteForge#Toolkit#Configuration#IConfigSection#SaveToConfiguration
member.summary: Saves the current values of the section's properties to the configuration root.
member.remarks: This method persists all properties of the object that are both readable and writable, unless they are marked with the . Properties are saved under their corresponding configuration keys, which are determined by the if present, or the property name otherwise. Array properties are stored in a dedicated configuration section, and their entries are cleared and rewritten during the save operation. Default values, as determined by , are not persisted to the configuration.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.ConfigSection`1.ThreadUnsafeSaveToConfiguration
member.summary: Saves the current object's properties to the configuration storage.
member.remarks:
This method persists all properties of the object that are both readable and writable, unless they are marked with the . Properties are saved under their corresponding configuration keys, which are determined by the if present, or the property name otherwise. Array properties are stored in a dedicated configuration section, and their entries are cleared and rewritten during the save operation. Default values, as determined by , are not persisted to the configuration.
Note: This method is not thread-safe.
Ensure that concurrent access is managed appropriately when calling this method.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.GlobalizationInfo
member.summary: Provides globalization information such as culture, date format, and time format.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.GlobalizationInfo.CultureInfo
member.summary: Gets or sets the culture information used for formatting.
member.remarks:
The property determines the culture-specific formatting for all values in this class.
For example, setting this to CultureInfo.GetCultureInfo("fr-FR") will use French formatting conventions.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.GlobalizationInfo.DateFormat
member.summary: Gets or sets the date format string.
member.remarks:
The format string should follow the standard .NET date and time format patterns.
For example, "MM/dd/yyyy" represents a date in month/day/year format.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.GlobalizationInfo.LongDateFormat
member.summary: Gets or sets the long date format string.
member.remarks:
The format string should follow the standard .NET date and time format patterns.
For example, "dddd, MMMM d, yyyy" represents a long date with day of week, month, day, and year.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.GlobalizationInfo.DateTimeFormat
member.summary: Gets the combined date and time format string.
member.remarks: This property combines and to provide a full date and time format string.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.GlobalizationInfo.ShortDateTimeFormat
member.summary: Gets the combined short date and short time format string.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.GlobalizationInfo.LongDateTimeFormat
member.summary: Gets the combined long date and long time format string.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.GlobalizationInfo.ShortDateTime12Format
member.summary: Gets the combined short date and short 12-hour time format string.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.GlobalizationInfo.LongDateTime12Format
member.summary: Gets the combined long date and long 12-hour time format string.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.GlobalizationInfo.LongTimeFormat
member.summary: Gets or sets the long time format string.
member.remarks:
The format string should follow the standard .NET date and time format patterns.
For example, "HH:mm:ss.fff" represents hours, minutes, seconds, and milliseconds in 24-hour format.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.GlobalizationInfo.ShortTimeFormat
member.summary: Gets or sets the short time format string.
member.remarks:
The format string should follow the standard .NET date and time format patterns.
For example, "HH:mm" represents hours and minutes in 24-hour format.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.GlobalizationInfo.TimeFormat
member.summary: Gets or sets the time format string.
member.remarks:
The format string should follow the standard .NET date and time format patterns.
For example, "HH:mm:ss" represents hours, minutes, and seconds in 24-hour format.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.GlobalizationInfo.LongTime12Format
member.summary: Gets or sets the long 12-hour time format string.
member.remarks:
The format string should follow the standard .NET date and time format patterns.
For example, "hh:mm:ss.fff tt" represents hours, minutes, seconds, milliseconds, and an AM/PM designator.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.GlobalizationInfo.ShortTime12Format
member.summary: Gets or sets the short 12-hour time format string.
member.remarks:
The format string should follow the standard .NET date and time format patterns.
For example, "h:mm tt" represents hours and minutes with an AM/PM designator.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.GlobalizationInfo.Time12Format
member.summary: Gets or sets the format string used to display time in a 12-hour clock format.
member.remarks:
The format string should follow the standard .NET date and time format patterns.
For example, "hh:mm tt" represents hours and minutes with an AM/PM designator.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.GlobalizationInfo.CurrencyFormat
member.summary: Gets or sets the currency format string.
member.remarks:
The format string should follow the standard .NET numeric format patterns.
For example, "'$' #,##0.00" represents a currency value with a dollar sign and two decimal places.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.GlobalizationInfo.IntegerFormat
member.summary: Gets or sets the format string used for displaying integers.
member.remarks:
The format string should follow the standard .NET numeric format patterns.
For example, "#,##0" represents an integer with thousand separators.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.GlobalizationInfo.NumberFormat
member.summary: Gets or sets the number format string.
member.remarks:
The format string should follow the standard .NET numeric format patterns.
For example, "#,##0.00" represents a number with thousand separators and two decimal places.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Configuration.GlobalizationInfo.PercentFormat
member.summary: Gets or sets the percent format string.
member.remarks:
The format string should follow the standard .NET numeric format patterns.
For example, "#0.00'%'" represents a percent value with two decimal places and a percent sign.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Core.Core
member.summary: Provides core helper functionality for the toolkit.
member.remarks: Currently exposes APIs for ensuring that embedded WinSCP distribution files (WinSCP.exe and WinSCPnet.dll) are extracted and available alongside the executing assembly.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Core.Core.EnsureWinSCP
member.summary: Ensures that the WinSCP executable and its .NET wrapper library are present on disk next to the entry assembly, extracting them from embedded resources if required.
member.remarks: Extraction occurs only when a file is missing or its checksum differs from the embedded resource.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Core.Core.WinScpResourceManager
member.summary: Manages extraction, validation (via SHA-256 checksum) and cleanup of embedded WinSCP resources.
member.remarks: This class is internal to the logic and not intended for external consumption. It compares the checksum of existing files to the embedded resource to avoid unnecessary writes.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Core.Core.WinScpResourceManager.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class. Determines the execution directory and the assembly containing embedded resources.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Core.Core.WinScpResourceManager.EnsureWinScpFilesAvailable
member.summary: Ensures both WinSCP related files exist and are up-to-date, extracting when necessary.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Core.Core.WinScpResourceManager.EnsureFileAvailable(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Validates (via checksum) whether a target file exists and matches the embedded resource; extracts the resource if validation fails.
member.param: Fully qualified embedded resource name.
Target output file name.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Core.Core.WinScpResourceManager.IsFileUpToDate(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Determines if an existing file matches the embedded resource by comparing SHA-256 checksums.
member.param: Embedded resource name.
Path to the existing file on disk.
member.returns: true if the file matches; otherwise false.
member.remarks: Any exception during comparison (e.g. missing resource, IO issues) results in a false return.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Core.Core.WinScpResourceManager.CalculateResourceChecksum(System.String)
member.summary: Computes the SHA-256 checksum of the embedded resource.
member.param: Name of the embedded resource.
member.returns: Byte array containing the checksum.
member.exception: Thrown when the embedded resource cannot be found.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Core.Core.WinScpResourceManager.CalculateFileChecksum(System.String)
member.summary: Computes the SHA-256 checksum of a file located on disk.
member.param: Path to the file to hash.
member.returns: Byte array containing the checksum.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Core.Core.WinScpResourceManager.CalculateChecksum(System.IO.Stream)
member.summary: Computes the SHA-256 hash for the provided stream.
member.param: Input stream to hash. Stream position should be at the beginning.
member.returns: Byte array containing the SHA-256 hash.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Core.Core.WinScpResourceManager.CompareChecksums(System.Byte[],System.Byte[])
member.summary: Compares two checksum byte arrays for equality.
member.param: First checksum.
Second checksum.
member.returns: true if lengths match and all bytes are equal; otherwise false.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Core.Core.WinScpResourceManager.ExtractResourceToFile(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Extracts an embedded resource to a specified file path using a safe temp file + move pattern.
member.param: Embedded resource to extract.
Destination path on disk.
member.exception: Thrown when extraction fails or the resource cannot be found.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Core.Core.WinScpResourceManager.AreWinScpFilesAvailable
member.summary: Determines whether both WinSCP related files exist and match the embedded resources.
member.returns: true if both files are present and up-to-date; otherwise false.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Core.Core.WinScpResourceManager.CleanupWinScpFiles
member.summary: Attempts to delete the extracted WinSCP files, ignoring any failures.

It should return to continue processing further rows, or to stop.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.ICSVRecord
member.summary: Represents a record in a CSV file with validation capabilities.
member.remarks:
This interface defines the core contract for CSV record objects in the ByteForge CSV processing framework. Classes implementing this interface represent a single row of data from a CSV file, with properties typically mapped to columns using the .
The interface focuses on validation capabilities, allowing implementing classes to define custom validation rules and track validation errors. This is particularly useful when processing CSV files that need to meet specific business or data format rules.
Classes implementing this interface are typically used with . The CSV reader will create instances of the record class and populate them with data from the CSV file.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.ICSVRecord.ValidationErrors
member.summary: Gets the validation errors associated with the record.
member.remarks:
This property provides access to a collection of validation error messages that describe why the record is invalid. The collection should be populated during the method call and can be used to provide detailed feedback about validation failures.
Implementations should typically initialize this property with a new instance of in their constructors to ensure it's never null.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.ICSVRecord.IsValid
member.summary: Determines whether the record is valid according to business rules.
member.returns: if the record is valid; otherwise, .
member.remarks:
This method evaluates the record against validation rules defined in the method. If validation fails, the method should add appropriate error messages to the collection before returning .
Implementations should typically clear any existing validation errors at the beginning of this method to ensure a clean validation state for each call.
Common validation checks include:
Checking for required fields (non-null, non-empty)
Validating data formats (emails, phone numbers, dates, etc.)
Range validation for numeric fields
Cross-field validation (dependencies between fields)

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.ICSVRecord.Validate
member.summary: Validates the current state of the object to ensure it meets the required conditions.
member.remarks:
This method checks the object's state and throws an exception if any validation rules are violated.
Call this method before performing operations that depend on the object's validity.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.BulkDbProcessor`1
member.summary:
Provides generic batch database operations for bulk inserting, upserting, and deleting records of type .
Supports SQL Server bulk operations and dynamic table creation based on property mappings.
member.typeparam: The type of records to be processed.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.BulkDbProcessor`1.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified destination table name.
member.param: The name of the destination table where data will be processed.
member.exception: Thrown if is null, empty, or consists only of whitespace.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.BulkDbProcessor`1.BatchSize
member.summary: Gets the batch size for bulk insert operations. Default is 1000 records.
member.remarks: This property determines how many records are sent to the database in a single batch during bulk operations. Can be overridden in derived classes to customize batch sizing.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.BulkDbProcessor`1.NullStringsAreEmpty
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether null strings should be converted to empty strings during processing.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.BulkDbProcessor`1.CreateDestinationTable
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the destination table should be created.
member.value: if the destination table should be created; otherwise, . Default is .
member.remarks: When , the processor will create the destination table if it doesn't already exist. When , the processor assumes the table already exists in the database.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.BulkDbProcessor`1.DropDestinationTableIfExists
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the destination table should be dropped if it already exists.
member.value: if the destination table should be dropped if it already exists; otherwise, . Default is .
member.remarks: When , the processor will drop the destination table if it already exists and recreate it. When , the processor will use the existing table structure. Setting this property to automatically sets to .

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.BulkDbProcessor`1.BulkCopyTimeout
member.summary: Gets the timeout in seconds for bulk copy operations. Default is 600 seconds (10 minutes).
member.remarks: This property controls how long SqlBulkCopy operations will wait before timing out. Can be overridden in derived classes to customize timeout behavior.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.BulkDbProcessor`1.ColumnMap
member.summary: Gets the cached property to column mapping.
member.remarks: This dictionary maps PropertyInfo objects to their corresponding database column names. It is initialized by based on DBColumnAttribute settings.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.BulkDbProcessor`1.DestinationTableName
member.summary: Gets the name of the destination table for bulk insert operations.
member.value: The name of the destination table in the database.
member.remarks: This is the table where records will be inserted, updated, or deleted from during bulk operations.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.BulkDbProcessor`1.Indexes
member.summary: Gets the names of the index columns.
member.value: An array of column names that have regular (non-unique) indexes.
member.remarks: These are determined from properties with set to , excluding those that are already part of primary keys or unique indexes.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.BulkDbProcessor`1.Properties
member.summary: Gets the cached properties for the current mapping.
member.value: An array of PropertyInfo objects for properties decorated with .
member.remarks: These properties are used to map between entity properties and database columns.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.BulkDbProcessor`1.PrimaryKeys
member.summary: Gets the names of the primary key columns.
member.value: An array of column names that form the primary key of the table.
member.remarks: These are determined from properties with set to .

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.BulkDbProcessor`1.UniqueIndexes
member.summary: Gets the names of the unique index columns.
member.value: An array of column names that have unique indexes but are not part of the primary key.
member.remarks: These are determined from properties with set to that are not also primary keys.

member.summary: Gets the most recent exception encountered during the operation of the application.
member.remarks: This property is updated whenever an exception is caught and logged by the application. It can be used to inspect the details of the most recent error for debugging or logging purposes.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DBAccess
member.summary: Provides a comprehensive set of methods for interacting with a database. Supports executing SQL queries, scripts, and retrieving data in various forms such as scalar values, records, and result sets. Handles database connections, command creation, parameter management, and logging of query execution details. Includes utility methods for type conversion and timing the execution of database operations. Designed to work with SQL Server and ODBC databases, and can be configured using a configuration file.
Provides methods for managing SQL Server extended properties on database objects. Extended properties allow storing descriptive metadata with database objects such as tables, columns, and procedures.
Partial class for DBAccess to support transaction management.

member.returns:
A list of parameter names found in the query string.
The list may include duplicates if the class is set to operate in .
If no parameters are found, an empty list is returned.
member.remarks:
This method removes SQL comments (both block comments and single-line comments) before extracting parameter names. Parameters within string literals are ignored. Parameter names are case-insensitive and must start with '@', followed by a letter and then alphanumeric characters or underscores.
For SQL Server databases, this method recognizes named parameter assignment syntax (e.g., '@paramName = @valueParam') and only includes the value parameters (@valueParam) in the result, treating the parameter names (@paramName) as literal SQL text.

It returns a object containing detailed information about the execution, including success status, result sets, and any exceptions encountered.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DatabaseOptions
member.summary: Represents the configuration options for a database connection.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DatabaseOptions.DatabaseType
member.summary: Gets or sets the type of database (SQLServer or ODBC).
member.remarks: This determines which connection string format will be used when is called.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DatabaseOptions.Server
member.summary: Gets or sets the database server name or IP.
member.remarks: For SQL Server connections, this is typically a server name, instance name, or IP address. For example: "localhost", "SQLSERVER01\INSTANCE1", or "192.168.1.100".

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DatabaseOptions.ServerDSN
member.summary: Gets or sets the database server DSN (for ODBC connections).
member.remarks: The Data Source Name (DSN) is only used when is set to . It should reference a system DSN configured in the ODBC Data Source Administrator.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DatabaseOptions.DatabaseName
member.summary: Gets or sets the database name.
member.remarks: Specifies the name of the database to connect to on the server.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DatabaseOptions.EncryptedUser
member.summary: Gets or sets the encrypted database user.
member.remarks:
This property stores the encrypted username. To access the decrypted value, use the property.
The value is encrypted using the class with predefined encryption keys.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DatabaseOptions.EncryptedPassword
member.summary: Gets or sets the encrypted database password.
member.remarks:
This property stores the encrypted password. To access the decrypted value, use the property.
The value is encrypted using the class with predefined encryption keys.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DatabaseOptions.ConnectionString
member.summary: Gets or sets the direct connection string (if provided).
member.remarks:
If this property is set, it will be used directly instead of constructing a connection string from the individual properties. This allows for custom connection strings that might need additional parameters not exposed by this class.
Note that when using a direct connection string, credentials may be stored in plain text, so use this with caution in scenarios requiring high security.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DatabaseOptions.UseEncryption
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the connection should be encrypted.
member.remarks:
For SQL Server connections, determines whether to use TLS/SSL encryption for the database connection. When true, the "Encrypt=Optional" parameter is added to the connection string.
This property affects the transport-level encryption of the database connection and is separate from the encryption of credentials in configuration storage.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DatabaseOptions.ConnectionTimeout
member.summary: Gets or sets the connection timeout duration in seconds.
member.value: The connection timeout duration in seconds. Defaults to 60 seconds.
member.remarks: Determines how long to wait for a connection to be established before timing out. This is particularly important in scenarios where network connectivity might be unreliable.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DatabaseOptions.CommandTimeout
member.summary: Gets or sets the command timeout duration in seconds.
member.value: The command timeout duration in seconds. Defaults to 240 seconds.
member.remarks:
Determines how long to wait for a command (query) to execute before timing out. This timeout applies to individual SQL commands rather than the initial connection establishment.
The default of 240 seconds (4 minutes) is suitable for most operations, but may need to be increased for long-running reports or data imports.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DatabaseOptions.UseTrustedConnection
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the connection uses a trusted connection.
member.value: if the connection uses a trusted connection; otherwise, . Defaults to .
member.remarks:
When set to true, Windows Authentication (integrated security) is used instead of SQL Server Authentication. In this case, the current Windows identity is used for authentication and the username/password properties are ignored.
This is often preferred in intranet scenarios where domain authentication is available and provides better security than storing credentials.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DatabaseOptions.AutoTrimStrings
member.summary: Gets or sets whether string values should be automatically trimmed.
member.value: if string values should be auto-trimmed; otherwise, . Defaults to .
member.remarks: When enabled, string values stored to the database will be automatically trimmed of leading and trailing whitespace.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DatabaseOptions.AllowNullStrings
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether null strings are allowed.
member.value: if null strings are allowed; otherwise, . Defaults to ."
member.remarks: When enabled, string properties can be set to null and will be stored as NULL in the database.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DatabaseOptions.User
member.summary: Gets or sets the database user name in a clear format.
member.remarks:
Accessing this property decrypts the stored encrypted user name.
Setting this property encrypts the provided value before storing it.
Thread safety is ensured by locking during both get and set operations.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DatabaseOptions.Password
member.summary: Gets or sets the password in an encrypted form.
member.remarks:
Accessing this property decrypts the stored encrypted password.
Setting this property encrypts the provided value before storing it.
Thread safety is ensured by locking during both get and set operations.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DatabaseOptions.GetConnectionString
member.summary: Gets the complete connection string based on the configuration options.
member.returns: A connection string suitable for database connection.
member.remarks:
This method generates a properly formatted connection string based on the database type and configuration settings. It handles the following cases:
If is provided, it's used directly
For SQL Server, builds a connection string with appropriate parameters
For ODBC, builds a DSN-based connection string
Sensitive information (username and password) is automatically decrypted during connection string generation.
member.exception: Thrown when an unsupported database type is specified.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DatabaseRootOptions
member.summary: Represents the root database configuration options.
member.remarks:
This class is used to determine which database configuration section should be used when multiple database configurations are defined in the configuration file.
It typically appears at the root level of configuration and references a section name that contains the actual database configuration options.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DatabaseRootOptions.SelectedDatabase
member.summary: Gets or sets the selected database configuration section.
member.remarks: This property contains the name of the configuration section that holds the database connection details. The value typically corresponds to an environment name like "Development", "Testing", or "Production".

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DBTransaction
member.summary: Represents a database transaction context for use with . Supports nested transactions via a stack model.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DBTransaction.AutoCommit
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether changes are automatically committed to the data source.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DBTransaction.Id
member.summary: Gets the unique identifier for this transaction instance.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DBTransaction.Commit
member.summary: Commits the transaction.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DBTransaction.Rollback
member.summary: Rolls back the current transaction, discarding any changes made within it.
member.remarks: This method marks the transaction as completed to prevent it from being automatically committed when the object is disposed. After calling this method, the transaction is removed from the transaction stack.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.DBTransaction.Dispose
member.summary: Disposes the transaction, rolling back if not committed and rollback is required.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.ScriptExecutionResult
member.summary: Represents the comprehensive result of executing a SQL script, potentially containing multiple batches.
member.remarks:
This class encapsulates all relevant information about the execution of a SQL script, including success status, result sets, affected record counts, and any exceptions that occurred.
SQL scripts can contain multiple batches (separated by GO statements in T-SQL), and this class tracks the results of each batch separately in the and collections.
This class is typically returned by the methods that execute SQL scripts, such as ExecuteScript or ExecuteScriptFile.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.ScriptExecutionResult.Success
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the script executed successfully.
member.remarks:
A value of indicates that all batches in the script executed without throwing exceptions. Note that this does not necessarily mean that the script accomplished its intended business logic - it only indicates that no SQL errors occurred.
When , the property will contain information about the error that caused the failure.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.ScriptExecutionResult.BatchResults
member.summary: Gets or sets the results of each batch executed in the script.
member.remarks:
This collection contains the raw return value from each batch in the script. For SELECT statements, this is typically the number of rows returned. For INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE statements, this is the number of rows affected.
The collection maintains the same order as the batches in the script.
Note that these values may be redundant with information in or , depending on the type of statements executed.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.ScriptExecutionResult.ResultSets
member.summary: Gets or sets the result sets returned by the script.
member.remarks:
This collection contains objects representing the result sets returned by SELECT statements in the script.
The collection maintains the same order as the SELECT statements in the script. Note that only SELECT statements generate result sets, so this collection may have fewer items than if the script contains other types of statements.
Each contains the columns and rows returned by the corresponding SELECT statement.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.ScriptExecutionResult.RecordsAffected
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of records affected by each batch in the script.
member.remarks:
This collection contains the number of rows affected by each batch in the script. For INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE statements, this is the number of rows that were modified by the operation.
The collection maintains the same order as the batches in the script.
For SELECT statements, the corresponding value may be -1 or the number of rows returned, depending on the database provider.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Data.ScriptExecutionResult.LastException
member.summary: Gets or sets the last exception that occurred during script execution.
member.remarks:
If is , this property contains the exception that caused the script execution to fail.
The exception typically includes information such as the error code, error message, and stack trace, which can be used to diagnose the cause of the failure.
This property will be null if is .

member.summary: Configuration options for the FileLogger.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.FileLoggerOptions.UseDaily
member.summary: Gets or sets whether to create a new log file each day.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.FileLoggerOptions.FileEncoding
member.summary: Gets or sets the encoding used for writing to the log file.
member.remarks: This property determines the character encoding applied when writing log entries to the file. Use this to specify an encoding such as or based on the requirements of the log file consumers.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.FileLoggerOptions.MaxFileSizeMB
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum file size in megabytes before rolling over (0 for unlimited).

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.FileLoggerOptions.FileNamingPattern
member.summary:
Gets or sets the file naming pattern for log files. Supports the following placeholders:
{basename} - Original filename without extension
{date:format} - Current date using specified format (e.g., {date:yyyy-MM-dd})
{timestamp} - Current timestamp (yyyyMMdd-HHmmss)
{index} - File index for size-based rotation
{pid} - Process ID
{guid} - Short GUID (8 characters)
The default is "{basename}" (no pattern).

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.FileLoggerOptions.CustomFileNameProvider
member.summary: Gets or sets a custom file name provider function. When set, this function is called to generate the file name and overrides FileNamingPattern. The function receives the base file path and current FileLoggerOptions as parameters.

member.summary: Logs that contain extremely detailed messages, typically showing every step of program execution. These messages are primarily for tracing code execution and may contain sensitive application data.

member.name: F:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.LogLevel.Debug
member.summary: Logs that are used for interactive investigation during development. These logs should primarily contain information useful for debugging and have no long-term value.

member.name: F:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.LogLevel.Verbose
member.summary: Logs that are detailed and contain extended information beyond what's necessary for basic application monitoring. While less detailed than Trace, these logs still provide significant granularity for troubleshooting.

member.name: F:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.LogLevel.Info
member.summary: Logs that track the general flow of the application. These logs should have long-term value and provide context for understanding normal application behavior.

member.name: F:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.LogLevel.Notice
member.summary: Logs that highlight noteworthy events that are still considered normal operation. These events might deserve special handling or attention but don't represent a problem condition.

member.name: F:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.LogLevel.Warning
member.summary: Logs that highlight abnormal or unexpected events in the application flow. These represent potential problems or situations that might lead to errors if not addressed, but do not cause the application execution to stop.

member.name: F:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.LogLevel.Error
member.summary: Logs that highlight when the current flow of execution is stopped due to a failure. These indicate a failure in the current activity or operation, but not an application-wide failure. The application can typically continue running despite the error.

member.name: F:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.LogLevel.Critical
member.summary: Logs that describe severe error conditions that might still allow the application to continue running, but require immediate attention and likely intervention to prevent system-wide failures.

member.name: F:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.LogLevel.Fatal
member.summary: Logs that describe unrecoverable conditions that result in immediate application termination. These represent the most severe error conditions that prevent the application from continuing to run.

member.name: F:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.LogLevel.None
member.summary: Specifies that no logging should be performed.

member.name: F:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.LogLevel.All
member.summary: Specifies that logging should be performed at all defined levels.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.LogSettings
member.summary: Represents the options for configuring the logging system.
member.remarks:
This class provides configuration settings that control how the system behaves, including where log files are stored, what level of detail is included in logs, and whether logs are cleared on application startup.
LogOptions is typically loaded from configuration files using the system, with property names mapped through .
The logging system supports multiple logging targets (console, file, etc.) which can be configured through this options class.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.LogSettings.LogFilePath
member.summary: Gets or sets the path to the log file.
member.remarks:
The default log file path is determined by the method, which creates a log file in the same directory as the executing assembly, with the same name as the assembly but with a .log extension.
When this property is not explicitly set in configuration, the method is called to provide a default value.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.LogSettings.TraceLogLevel
member.summary: Gets or sets the log level for tracing.
member.remarks:
Determines the minimum severity level of messages that will be logged. Messages with a severity level less than this value will be ignored.
The levels, in increasing order of severity, are:
- Very detailed messages for tracing execution
- Debugging information
- Very detailed information
- Normal application flow information
- Potential issues that don't stop execution
- Errors that allow continued execution
- Critical errors that stop execution
- Disables all logging
- Enables all logging levels
For example, setting this to will log Info, Warning, Error, and Fatal messages, but ignore Trace and Debug messages.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.LogSettings.ClearLogOnStartup
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the log should be cleared on startup.
member.value: if the log should be cleared on startup; otherwise, .
member.remarks:
When set to true, the log file will be cleared (truncated to zero length) when the application starts. This is useful for keeping logs from growing too large over time, but means that information from previous runs will be lost.
For production systems, it's often better to set this to false and implement a log rotation strategy instead.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.LogSettings.UseSessionLogging
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to use session-based logging. When true, each application run gets its own unique log file.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.LogSettings.GetDefaultLogPath
member.summary: Gets the default log file path.
member.returns: The default log file path.
member.remarks:
This method is called automatically by the configuration system when the property is not explicitly set. It generates a default path based on the executing assembly's location and name.
The method attempts to get the entry assembly first (the main application), falling back to the calling assembly, and finally the executing assembly if neither of those are available.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.SessionFileLoggerOptions
member.summary: Configuration options for the . Inherits all file logger options and adds session-specific settings.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.SessionFileLoggerOptions.SessionIdFormat
member.summary: Gets or sets the format for generating session IDs. The default is . This is used to replace the {sessionid} placeholder in the FileNamingPattern.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.SessionFileLoggerOptions.WriteSessionHeader
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to write a session header with metadata at the beginning of the log file. The default is .

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.SessionFileLoggerOptions.WriteSessionFooter
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to write a session footer when closing the log file. The default is .

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.SessionFileLoggerOptions.CleanupOldSessions
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to automatically clean up old session files on startup, according to . The default is .

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.SessionFileLoggerOptions.CustomSessionIdProvider
member.summary: Gets or sets a custom session ID provider function. Only used when is set to . The returned value is used to replace the {sessionid} placeholder in the FileNamingPattern.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.SessionIdFormat
member.summary: Specifies the format for generating session IDs.

member.name: F:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.SessionIdFormat.Timestamp
member.summary: Uses timestamp format: yyyyMMdd-HHmmss.

member.name: F:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.SessionIdFormat.TimestampWithMilliseconds
member.summary: Uses timestamp with milliseconds: yyyyMMdd-HHmmss-fff.

member.name: F:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.SessionIdFormat.Guid
member.summary: Uses the first 8 characters of a GUID.

member.name: F:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.SessionIdFormat.ProcessId
member.summary: Uses timestamp with process ID: yyyyMMdd-HHmmss-P1234.

member.name: F:ByteForge.Toolkit.Logging.SessionIdFormat.Custom
member.summary: Uses a custom provider function for the session ID.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Mail.AttachmentProcessResult
member.summary: Encapsulates the complete result of an email attachment processing operation.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Mail.AttachmentProcessResult.ProcessingMethod
member.summary: Gets or sets the processing method used.
member.remarks: Indicates how the attachments were handled - whether they were attached directly, compressed into a single archive, split into multiple parts, or if processing failed. This can be used to determine how to structure the email subject and body text.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Mail.AttachmentProcessResult.SkippedFiles
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of files that were skipped during processing.
member.remarks:
If any files could not be processed (e.g., due to access restrictions or file corruption), they will be included here along with the reason they were skipped.
This information can be useful for reporting problems to users or for retry logic.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Mail.AttachmentProcessResult.PartDistribution
member.summary: Gets or sets the distribution of parts in a multi-part archive.
member.remarks:
When using , this provides information about which original files were included in each archive part.
This information can be useful for generating email content that explains the distribution of files across multiple parts.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Mail.AttachmentProcessResult.Error
member.summary: Gets or sets the error message if processing failed.
member.remarks:
If an error occurred during processing, this property will contain a descriptive message. If processing was successful, this will be null or empty.
A non-empty error message implies that will be false.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Mail.AttachmentProcessResult.Success
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the processing was successful.
member.remarks:
A simple way to check if processing completed without errors.
Even if is true, there may still be files in the list if some files were skipped but overall processing was still considered successful.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Mail.PartInfo
member.summary: Information about a part in a multi-part archive created during email attachment processing.
member.remarks:
When files are too large to be sent as a single attachment, they may be split into multiple archive parts. This class stores metadata about each part, including which files were included and the sequential part number.
PartInfo objects are typically created by the when using the method and are returned in the property.
This information can be useful for generating emails that describe which files are included in each archive part or for tracking the distribution of files across multiple message attachments.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Mail.PartInfo.PartNumber
member.summary: Gets or sets the part number.
member.remarks: The part number is a 1-based index that represents the sequence of this part in the multi-part archive. For example, part 1 of 3.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Mail.PartInfo.FileCount
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of files in the part.
member.remarks: This is a convenience property that returns the number of files listed in the collection. It represents the number of original files that were added to this part of the archive.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Mail.PartInfo.Files
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of file names in the part.
member.remarks: Contains the names of the original files that were included in this part of the archive. These are not the archive files themselves, but rather the source files that were compressed into this archive part.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Security.AESEncryption
member.summary:
The AESEncryption class provides methods for encrypting and decrypting data using the AES (Advanced Encryption Standard) algorithm.
It includes methods for shifting and rotating values, packing and unpacking bytes, generating keys, and performing byte substitutions and transformations.

member.returns: The result of the right shift operation.

member.summary: Performs a left rotation operation on a long value.

The number of bits to rotate to the left.
member.returns: The byte value after the bits have been rotated to the left.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Security.AESEncryption.XTime(System.Byte)
member.summary: Performs a specific byte operation used in AES encryption.
member.param: The byte to be processed.
member.returns: The result of the operation.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Security.AESEncryption.ByteMultiplication(System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.summary: Multiplies two bytes in the Galois Field(2^8) used in AES.
member.param: The first byte to be multiplied.
The second byte to be multiplied.
member.returns: The product of the two bytes in the Galois Field(2^8).
member.remarks:
A Galois Field, named after the mathematician Évariste Galois, is a finite field, meaning it contains a finite number of elements. In the context of cryptography, particularly in algorithms like AES (Advanced Encryption Standard), Galois Fields are used for mathematical operations.
The most commonly used Galois Field in cryptography is GF(2^8), which contains 256 elements and is therefore suitable for operations on bytes. In GF(2^8), addition and subtraction are equivalent to the XOR operation, and multiplication and division are more complex but can be implemented efficiently using lookup tables.
The use of Galois Fields in cryptography provides several benefits. The operations are reversible, which is necessary for decryption, and they provide good diffusion and confusion, which are desirable properties in a cipher. The structure of a Galois Field also allows for efficient implementation of the cipher in hardware or software.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Security.AESEncryption.ByteSustitutionBox(System.Int64)
member.summary: Performs a byte substitution on a long value using the forward substitution box.
member.param: The long value to be substituted.
member.returns: The substituted long value.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Security.AESEncryption.Product(System.Int64,System.Int64)
member.summary: Multiplies two long values in the Galois Field(2^8) used in AES.
member.param: The first long value to be multiplied.
The second long value to be multiplied.
member.returns: The product of the two long values in the Galois Field(2^8).

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Security.AESEncryption.InverseMixColumns(System.Int64)
member.summary: Performs the inverse MixColumns operation in AES, which operates on a column of four bytes at a time.
member.param: The long value representing the column of four bytes.
member.returns: The result of the inverse MixColumns operation.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Security.AESEncryption.SubstituteByte(System.Byte)
member.summary: Performs a byte substitution operation using a precomputed substitution table.
member.param: The byte to be substituted.
member.returns: The substituted byte.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Security.AESEncryption.GenTables
member.summary: Generates the substitution tables and other necessary data structures used in AES encryption and decryption.
member.remarks:
Substitution tables, also known as S-boxes, are used in various encryption algorithms, including AES (Advanced Encryption Standard). They are a fundamental part of substitution-permutation network (SPN) based ciphers.
An S-box is essentially a lookup table used to perform substitution in the process of encryption or decryption.In the context of AES, the S-box is a matrix of 16x16, giving a total of 256 possible values. Each byte of the state is replaced by the byte at the corresponding row and column in the S-box.
The purpose of the S-box is to provide non-linearity in the cipher, which helps to protect against linear and differential cryptanalysis.The specific values in the S-box are chosen to maximize the non-linearity and the avalanche effect (where a small change in input produces a significant change in output).
In the case of AES, the S-box is designed in such a way that there are no fixed points or opposite fixed points, which means that no byte value, when input to the S - box, will output itself or its bitwise complement. This contributes to the security of the cipher.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Security.AESEncryption.GKey(System.Int64,System.Int64,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Generates the round keys for AES encryption and decryption based on the input key.
member.param: The block size in 32-bit words. Usually 4 for AES.
The key size in 32-bit words. 4 for AES-128, 6 for AES-192, 8 for AES-256.
The input key used to generate the round keys.
member.remarks:
In the context of symmetric encryption algorithms like AES (Advanced Encryption Standard), round keys are derived from the original encryption key using a key schedule algorithm.
The original key is expanded into an array of keys, one for each round of the encryption process.These round keys are used in each round of the encryption and decryption process. The use of different keys in each round adds to the security of the algorithm, making it resistant to various types of cryptographic attacks.
In AES, the number of rounds and thus the number of round keys depends on the size of the original key.For example, AES-128 uses 10 rounds, AES-192 uses 12 rounds, and AES-256 uses 14 rounds.Each round key is the same size as the block size, which is 128 bits in AES.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Security.AESEncryption.EncryptBuffer(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Encrypts a buffer of bytes using the AES encryption algorithm. The buffer size should be a multiple of the block size (16 bytes for AES). The encryption is done in-place, meaning the original bytes in the buffer are replaced by the encrypted bytes.
member.param: The buffer of bytes to be encrypted.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Security.AESEncryption.DecryptBuffer(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Decrypts a buffer of bytes using the AES decryption algorithm. The buffer size should be a multiple of the block size (16 bytes for AES). The decryption is done in-place, meaning the original bytes in the buffer are replaced by the decrypted bytes.
member.param: The buffer of bytes to be decrypted.

The password used for encryption, represented as a byte array.

member.summary: Decrypts an array of bytes using the specified password.

member.remarks: This class was ported from the original VBMath.vb from the official Microsoft.VisualBasic.Core library. https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/blob/6d0ac32a8e3a53e03b4143899844b587ebb19b49/src/libraries/Microsoft.VisualBasic.Core/src/Microsoft/VisualBasic/VBMath.vb

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Security.VBMath.Rnd
member.summary: Generates a random floating-point number between 0.0 and 1.0, equivalent to VB6's Rnd() with no arguments.
member.returns: A random float value in the range [0.0, 1.0).

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Security.VBMath.Rnd(System.Single)
member.summary: Generates a random floating-point number based on the provided number, equivalent to VB6's Rnd(Number). If Number is 0, returns the next random number from the current seed. If Number is negative, uses it to reseed the generator. If Number is positive, generates a new random number and updates the seed.
member.param: The input value to control random number generation. 0 for next random, negative for reseeding, positive for new random.
member.returns: A random float value in the range [0.0, 1.0).

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Security.VBMath.Randomize
member.summary: Initializes the random number generator with a seed based on the current time, equivalent to VB6's Randomize with no arguments.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Security.VBMath.Randomize(System.Double)
member.summary: Initializes the random number generator with a seed based on the provided double value, equivalent to VB6's Randomize(Number).
member.param: The double value used to generate the seed.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Utilities.BooleanParser
member.summary: Provides enhanced methods to parse string representations into values, with support for various common boolean representations beyond the standard "true" and "false".
member.remarks:
This class extends the functionality of the standard method by recognizing a wider range of string representations for boolean values.
By default, the following string values are recognized:
True values: "true", "t", "yes", "y", "1", "on", "enabled"
False values: "false", "f", "no", "n", "0", "off", "disabled"
The parser is case-insensitive, so "TRUE", "True", and "true" are all recognized as true.
Additionally, non-zero integer values are interpreted as true, and zero is interpreted as false.
The class also provides methods to register custom string representations for true and false values.

member.name: P:ByteForge.Toolkit.Utilities.BooleanParser.Default
member.summary: Gets the default instance of the class.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Utilities.BooleanParser.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with default true and false values.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Utilities.BooleanParser.ParseValue(System.String)
member.summary: Parses the specified string into a value.
member.param: The string to parse.
member.returns:
A value that represents the parsed string:
True if the input matches any value in the true values collection
False if the input matches any value in the false values collection
True if the input can be parsed as a non-zero integer
False if the input can be parsed as zero
False if the input is null
member.exception: Thrown when the input string cannot be parsed into a valid . This occurs when the input is not recognized as a boolean value and cannot be interpreted as a numeric value.
member.remarks:
This method is similar to but recognizes a wider range of string representations for boolean values.
The method first trims whitespace from the input and handles null inputs as false. It then checks if the input matches any known true or false values. If not, it attempts to parse the input as an integer, treating non-zero values as true.
If none of these approaches succeeds, a is thrown.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Utilities.BooleanParser.TryParseValue(System.String,System.Boolean@)
member.summary: Tries to parse the specified string into a value.
member.param: The string to parse.

When this method returns, contains the value equivalent to the string contained in , if the conversion succeeded, or if the conversion failed. The conversion fails if the parameter is null or does not contain a valid string representation of a boolean value.
member.returns: if the parameter was converted successfully; otherwise, .
member.remarks:
This method is similar to but recognizes a wider range of string representations for boolean values.
Unlike , this method does not throw an exception when the conversion fails. Instead, it returns and sets to .
This method internally calls and catches any exceptions that might occur.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Utilities.BooleanParser.AddTrueValue(System.String)
member.summary: Registers a new string value to be recognized as true.
member.param: The string value to register.
member.exception: Thrown when the value is null.
Thrown when the value is already registered as false.
member.remarks:
This method allows you to extend the set of string representations that are recognized as true. This can be useful for internationalization or supporting domain-specific boolean representations.
The value is automatically converted to lowercase and trimmed of whitespace before being added to the set of recognized true values.
If the value is already in the set of recognized true values, this method has no effect.
If the value is in the set of recognized false values, an is thrown to prevent ambiguity.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Utilities.BooleanParser.AddFalseValue(System.String)
member.summary: Registers a new string value to be recognized as false.
member.param: The string value to register.
member.exception: Thrown when the value is null.
Thrown when the value is already registered as true.
member.remarks:
This method allows you to extend the set of string representations that are recognized as false. This can be useful for internationalization or supporting domain-specific boolean representations.
The value is automatically converted to lowercase and trimmed of whitespace before being added to the set of recognized false values.
If the value is already in the set of recognized false values, this method has no effect.
If the value is in the set of recognized true values, an is thrown to prevent ambiguity.

member.returns: True if the value was removed; false if it was not present.
member.exception: Thrown when the value is null.

member.exception: Thrown when the value is null.

member.summary: Centers text in the console window.
member.param: The text to center.
member.remarks: If the text is longer than the console width, it will be displayed without centering. This method does nothing if the console is not available.

member.summary: Right-aligns text in the console window.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Utilities.ConsoleUtil.DrawProgressBar(System.Double,System.String)
member.summary: Draws a progress bar on the console.
member.param: The percentage of completion (0 to 100). Use negative values for indeterminate progress.
An optional message to display alongside the progress bar.
member.remarks: This method draws a filled progress bar representing the percentage of completion. When is negative, an indeterminate/animated progress bar is shown instead. The progress bar occupies approximately 30% of the console width. This method does nothing if the console is not available.
member.testing: Note: Due to the nature of console output, automated testing of this method is not feasible. It would require capturing and validating console output, which is not practical in a unit test environment.

member.summary: Builds a determinate progress bar string based on the given percentage.
member.param: Reference to the percentage value, which will be clamped between 0 and 100.
Optional message to display with the progress bar.
Width of the progress bar in characters.
member.returns: A formatted string representing the progress bar.

member.summary: Builds an indeterminate progress bar string with an animated block.
member.param: Optional message to display with the progress bar.
Width of the progress bar in characters.
member.returns: A formatted string representing the animated progress bar.

member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the console is available for input and output operations.
member.value: if the console is available; otherwise, .
member.remarks:
This property can be used to determine if the application has access to a functional console environment.
It returns in scenarios such as when the application is running in a non-console context (e.g., a GUI application) or when output streams are redirected.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Utilities.ConsoleUtil.GetConsoleWindow
member.summary: Retrieves the handle to the console window associated with the calling process.
member.returns: A handle to the console window, or if the calling process does not have a console window.
member.remarks:
This method is a platform invocation (P/Invoke) call to the Windows API function GetConsoleWindow.
It is only supported on Windows operating systems.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Utilities.DateTimeParser
member.summary: Provides methods to parse date and time strings into objects.
member.remarks:
This class provides enhanced date and time parsing capabilities, supporting a wide variety of formats beyond the standard .NET parsing functions. It intelligently attempts multiple formats and caches successful patterns for improved performance.
The class can be used through static methods (which use a shared singleton instance) or by creating instance(s) with potentially different configurations.

member.summary: Gets the files from the specified path that match the search pattern.
member.param: The directory path to search in.
The search pattern to match against the names of files in the path. Multiple patterns can be separated by a semicolon (;).
Specifies whether to search the current directory, or the current directory and all subdirectories. The default is .
member.returns: An array of the full names (including paths) for the files in the specified directory that match the search pattern.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Utilities.IOUtils.GetFileInfos(System.String,System.String,System.IO.SearchOption)
member.summary: Retrieves an array of objects that match the specified search pattern in the given directory.
member.param: The path to the directory to search. This cannot be null or empty.
A string containing one or more search patterns, separated by ';' or '|', to match against the names of files in the directory. Wildcards such as '*' and '?' are supported.
Specifies whether to search only the current directory or all subdirectories. The default is .
member.returns: An array of objects representing the files that match the specified search pattern. If no files match, an empty array is returned.
member.remarks: This method supports multiple search patterns separated by ';' or '|'. For example, a of "*.txt;*.csv" will match all files with a ".txt" or ".csv" extension.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Utilities.NameUtil
member.summary: Provides helper methods for normalizing and capitalizing personal names while respecting linguistic particles (e.g. "de", "van"), cultural prefixes (e.g. "Mc", "Mac", "O'"), hyphenated structures, and common edge cases.
member.remarks:
Rules implemented:
- Particles in remain lowercase unless they are the first token.
- Special prefixes in are normalized to their canonical casing.
- Prefixes "Mc"/"Mac" attempt intelligent second-letter capitalization with exception handling.
- Hyphenated components are capitalized individually.
- Handles apostrophe-based prefixes like "O'" and "D'".
- Preserves spacing normalization; multiple internal spaces collapse to single spaces.
member.example:
Examples:
NameUtil.CapitalizeName("mCdonald"); // "McDonald" NameUtil.CapitalizeName("sean o'connor"); // "Sean O'Connor" NameUtil.CapitalizeName("maria de la cruz"); // "Maria de la Cruz" NameUtil.CapitalizeFullName("smith, john de la"); // "Smith, John de la"

member.name: F:ByteForge.Toolkit.Utilities.NameUtil.LowercaseParticles
member.summary: Particles that should remain lowercase unless they are the first component in the name.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Utilities.NameUtil.CapitalizeFullName(System.String)

member.param: Raw full name string.
member.returns: Fully capitalized name or original if null/whitespace.

member.name: M:ByteForge.Toolkit.Utilities.NameUtil.NormalizeUserName(System.String)
member.summary: Normalizes a user name by trimming whitespace, converting to lowercase, and removing domain information.

member.name: T:ByteForge.Toolkit.Utilities.Parser
member.summary: Provides methods for parsing strings into various types and converting objects back to string representations.
member.remarks:
The offers:
Built-in support for primitive types, common framework types (e.g., , , ), and arrays of primitive characters and bytes.
Enum support via .
Fallback to JSON (de)serialization (Newtonsoft.Json) for unknown types.
Custom extensibility using .
Thread Safety: The instance is not thread-safe regarding type registration. If registration and parsing occur concurrently from multiple threads, external synchronization is required. Read-only usage after graph initialization is safe.

member.name: F:ByteForge.Toolkit.Utilities.Parser._default
member.summary: Stores the lazily created default singleton instance of the class.

member.name: F:ByteForge.Toolkit.Utilities.Parser._typeParsers
member.summary: Maps concrete instances to parsing delegates that convert a into an object instance.
member.remarks: This dictionary only contains explicitly registered or built-in known types. When a type is not found here (and is not an enum), JSON deserialization is attempted.

member.name: F:ByteForge.Toolkit.Utilities.Parser._typeStringifiers
member.summary: Maps concrete instances to delegates that convert an object instance into its canonical string representation.
member.remarks: For unknown types (and non-enum types) not present in this dictionary, JSON serialization is used as a fallback.
